name: Tower Bridge
description: Iconic Victorian bascule bridge over the Thames, built 1886-1894

name: Big Ben
description: The Elizabeth Tower housing the Great Bell, a London icon since 1859

name: British Museum
description: World-class museum of human history and culture, free admission

name: Hyde Park
description: One of London's Royal Parks, 350 acres of green space

name: St Paul's Cathedral
description: Wren's masterpiece, topped by one of the world's largest domes

name: Buckingham Palace
description: Official residence of the British monarch since 1837

name: Trafalgar Square
description: Public square commemorating the Battle of Trafalgar, with Nelson's Column

name: Camden Market
description: Eclectic market with street food, vintage clothes, and crafts

name: The Shard
description: Western Europe's tallest building, 310m glass-clad pyramid

name: Tower of London
description: Historic castle and fortress, home of the Crown Jewels

name: Natural History Museum
description: World-leading museum with 80 million specimens, free entry

name: Millennium Bridge
description: Steel suspension footbridge for pedestrians crossing the Thames

name: Greenwich Park
description: Royal Park with views of the city and home to the Prime Meridian

name: Westminster Abbey
description: Gothic abbey church, site of royal coronations since 1066

name: Piccadilly Circus
description: Famous road junction and public space in London's West End

name: Borough Market
description: One of London's oldest and largest food markets, dating to the 12th century

name: Tate Modern
description: Modern art gallery in a converted power station on the South Bank

name: Regent's Park
description: Royal Park with gardens, a lake, and London Zoo
